name: Route of the Faces - circular from Buendía (PR-CU 46)
name_original: Ruta de la Caras – circular desde Buendía (PR-CU 46)
region_intro: Hiking around Buendía offers diverse landscapes shaped by the Buendía Dam and the Guadiela River, creating a tranquil environment with picturesque waters. The region features extensive pine forests and distinctive sandstone formations, providing varied terrain for exploration. Hikers can expect a blend of riverine paths and forested trails, characteristic of the Spanish plateau. The area's geology contributes to unique natural features found along its routes. Best hiking trails around Buendía The most popular hiking route is Ermita de Nuestra Señora de los Desamparados – circular along the Guadiela River (PR-CU 47), an 8.0 miles (12.8 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the scenic Guadiela River to a historic hermitage. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Faces - circular from Buendía (PR-CU 46), a moderate 9.4 miles (15.2 km) path. This trail winds through pine forests, featuring allegorical sculptures carved into sandstone rocks. Local hikers also love the Route of the Faces loop from Aparcamiento Ruta de Las Caras, a 1.0 mile (1.6 km) trail leading through pine forests to unique sandstone sculptures, often completed in about 26 minutes. Hiking around Buendía is defined by its serene riverine landscapes, extensive pine forests, and unique sandstone formations. The network offers options for easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Buendía are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Buendía's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Buendía?
A: Hiking around Buendía offers a diverse landscape. You'll find extensive pine forests, serene riverine paths along the Guadiela River, and unique sandstone formations. The area is characterized by a blend of natural beauty and distinctive geological features, providing varied scenery throughout your hikes.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking routes in Buendía?
A: Yes, Buendía offers routes suitable for various ability levels, including easy options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31443085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces loop from Aparcamiento Ruta de Las Caras&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 1.0-mile (1.6 km) trail that takes about 26 minutes to complete, making it ideal for families or those seeking a shorter walk.

Q: What unique natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Buendía?
A: Buendía is known for its unique attractions. A must-see is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3905216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces&lt;/a&gt;, where allegorical sculptures are carved into sandstone rocks within a pine forest. You can also visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3906128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buendía Dam&lt;/a&gt; and enjoy views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5398646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering picturesque vistas of the reservoir and surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Buendía?
A: Yes, many routes in Buendía are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012673371/ermita-de-nuestra-senora-de-los-desamparados-circular-along-the-guadiela-river-pr-cu-47" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Nuestra Señora de los Desamparados – circular along the Guadiela River (PR-CU 47)&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.0-mile (12.8 km) trail that follows the scenic Guadiela River.

Q: What is the overall hiking experience like in Buendía, according to other hikers?
A: The hiking experience in Buendía is highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the serene riverine landscapes, the beauty of the extensive pine forests, and the unique artistic element of the sandstone sculptures found along routes like the Route of the Faces.

Q: Can I hike with my dog on the trails around Buendía?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on every trail are not detailed, the natural and open character of the Buendía area, with its forests and riverbanks, generally makes it suitable for hiking with dogs. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and ensure they do not disturb wildlife or other hikers.

Q: What are the typical durations for hikes in Buendía?
A: Hikes in Buendía vary in length and duration. Shorter, easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31443085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces loop from Aparcamiento Ruta de Las Caras&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in under 30 minutes. Moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012671860/route-of-the-faces-circular-from-buendia-pr-cu-46" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces - circular from Buendía (PR-CU 46)&lt;/a&gt;, typically take around 4 hours to finish.

Q: Is there parking available for hikers in Buendía?
A: Yes, parking is generally available for popular hiking areas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31443085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically mentions starting from 'Aparcamiento Ruta de Las Caras', indicating dedicated parking facilities for this well-known trail.

Q: Are there any historical or religious sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012673371/ermita-de-nuestra-senora-de-los-desamparados-circular-along-the-guadiela-river-pr-cu-47" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Nuestra Señora de los Desamparados – circular along the Guadiela River (PR-CU 47)&lt;/a&gt; leads to the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3235056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of the Forsaken&lt;/a&gt;. This site, also known as 'El Sitio', is situated on the banks of the Guadiela River and is equipped with picnic areas, a drinking water fountain, and restrooms, making it a pleasant destination for a hike.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Buendía?
A: Buendía's diverse ecosystems, including pine forests and riverine landscapes, make it enjoyable across seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for hiking. In summer, the area around the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Forsaken even allows for a refreshing dip in the Guadiela River, combining hiking with swimming.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, particularly during the warmer months. The area around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3235056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of the Forsaken&lt;/a&gt;, located on the banks of the Guadiela River, is known to be a spot where visitors can take a dip in the waters during summer, offering a refreshing end to a hike.

Q: What kind of elevation changes can I expect on the trails?
A: The trails around Buendía generally feature moderate elevation changes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012673371/ermita-de-nuestra-senora-de-los-desamparados-circular-along-the-guadiela-river-pr-cu-47" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Nuestra Señora de los Desamparados – circular along the Guadiela River (PR-CU 47)&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of approximately 159 meters, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012671860/route-of-the-faces-circular-from-buendia-pr-cu-46" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces - circular from Buendía (PR-CU 46)&lt;/a&gt; involves about 121 meters of ascent, making most routes accessible without extreme climbs.
tips: The Buendía reservoir is located on the border between the provinces of Cuenca and Guadalajara and is one of the most important in the community of Castilla La Mancha. On the route of the faces is this viewpoint with beautiful views towards it.

Free access tourist route located near the municipality of Buendía, Cuenca, among pine forests, sandstone abounds. In them you will find the Route of the Faces, a hiking route of a couple of kilometers in which you will see yourself accompanied by pine trees and allegorical sculptures.
Faces sculpted in sandstone rock of different sizes, some spectacular, with dimensions of four and six meters, which took eight years to sculpt due to the hardness of the rock.
http://www.rutadelascaras.com/
